questions.question: Differentiate between an Event Source and an Event Listener in the Java Delegation Event Model.
keyPoints: Event Source is the GUI component that generates an event (e.g., JButton).
Event Listener is an object that is notified when an event occurs.
The source must register the listener for notification to happen.
The listener implements a specific interface to handle the event type.

questions.question: Describe three different ways to implement an ActionListener for a JButton. Discuss one advantage of each approach.
keyPoints: Anonymous Inner Class: Implemented inline where the button is created. Advantage: Keeps code concise for simple actions.
Named Inner Class: A separate class within the outer class. Advantage: Better organization and reusability for complex logic within the same frame.
Container Class Implementation: The main JFrame implements ActionListener. Advantage: Centralizes all event handling code in one place. (Mention disadvantage: can lead to large if-else blocks).
Using Lambda Expressions: (Java 8+) Very concise syntax. Advantage: Reduces boilerplate code significantly for functional interfaces.
